country_risk_title: Executive Overreach and Concentration of Power Undermining Democratic Institutions
connection_summary: The disruption of a project activity by parliamentary policy hearings reflects the broader pattern of weak institutional coordination and governance dysfunction in Sierra Leone. Where executive power is concentrated and public institutions are instrumentalized for political purposes, legislative and executive processes can operate in ways that crowd out or override development program schedules without adequate coordination mechanisms. The postponement is consistent with an environment where institutional processes are unpredictable and not well-coordinated with development partners.

country_risk_title: Executive Overreach and Concentration of Power Undermining Democratic Institutions
connection_summary: The tension between Chiefdom authorities and District Councils over revenue sharing reflects a broader pattern of weak institutional delineation and patronage-driven governance in Sierra Leone. When central executive power is concentrated and public institutions are used as vehicles for patronage, sub-national governance arrangements—including the clarification of fiscal roles between traditional and elected local authorities—are likely to remain contested and under-resourced. The failure to operationalize clear MOUs under the Local Government Act is consistent with an environment where institutional mandates are subordinated to political and patronage interests rather than rule-based governance.

country_risk_title: Suppression of Civil Society and Erosion of Anti-Corruption Accountability
